pairs.fix: station 3 (draw-order.ts): explicit BSP draw order vs a centroid painter's sort
pairs.groundTruth: centroid sort inverts the stacked-eave overlaps (oracle: conflicts>0); BSP order is 0.

pairs.fix: station 3: a sail blade sweeping past the balcony railing — the catalogued inversion
pairs.groundTruth: centroid sort paints the far sail blade over the near railing (oracle: conflicts>0); BSP is 0.

pairs.fix: station 3: the ground-level door/windows sorting behind the wall they are cut into
pairs.groundTruth: centroid sort paints apertures/parts in the wrong depth order (oracle: conflicts>0); BSP is 0.

pairs.fix: the aperture fix (176aca90): compound-path opening + edge provenance vs stroked facade strips
pairs.groundTruth: stroked subdivision paints seams across the wall / a box around the door that are not real edges.

pairs.fix: the aperture fix: compound-path windows + edge provenance vs stroked facade strips
pairs.groundTruth: stroked subdivision draws grid seams across every pierced wall that are not real edges.

pairs.fix: the temple palette (warm timber vs cool slate) vs one flat blue mass
pairs.groundTruth: without the warm/cool contrast the tiered roof reads as one blue mass (owner call, #823).

pairs.fix: the 0.34 reveal (windows recessed with a shaded jamb) vs a flush pane
pairs.groundTruth: a flush pane reads as a decal stuck on the wall; 0.34 gives depth (reveal study, DEFAULT_REVEAL).

pairs.fix: the 0.34 reveal vs an over-deep 0.7 reveal
pairs.groundTruth: a 0.7 reveal becomes the thing you notice about the building; 0.34 was the owner-scoped depth.

pairs.fix: flaredRoof concave sweep (1.5) vs a straight-hip roof (sweep 1) — the "flat plates" kit gap
pairs.groundTruth: without the concave flaredRoof sweep the tiered roofs read as flat plates (kit gap, #824).

pairs.fix: N·L shading (facets read as solid form) vs flat plates (every face one tone) [synthetic control]
pairs.groundTruth: flat shading collapses the isometric solid into flat coloured plates with no read of depth.
